_comment: Phase-53 ESLINT-RATCHET-1 shrink-only baseline. The two propmanager-scoped rules (no-hardcoded-english-strings + no-ltr-class) are severity=error in eslint.config.js; the vuejs-accessibility rules stay severity=warn (dev-friendly) but are gated here. The watchdog (scripts/lint-baseline.mjs) runs ESLint, counts violations per rule, and exits non-zero if any current count exceeds its baseline below. Lower a value here ONLY after fixing real violations; never raise.

phase-53.rationale: Phase 44 codemod cleared bulk LTR-class residue (906 → 0). Hardcoded-English bulk remains in Vue templates; the baseline captures the residual + ratchets downward as Phase-43 + Phase-44 + Phase-52 i18n backfill commits land. Severity stays at warn so `npm run lint` is dev-workflow-friendly; the contract is enforced by `npm run lint:baseline` in CI — new violations grow the count past the baseline and fail the gate.

a11y-ratchet.rationale: The 9 vuejs-accessibility rules were severity=warn but UNGATED, so a11y warnings accumulated unchecked (1546 at lock time, led by label-has-for 743 + form-control-has-label 593). Locking them shrink-only here: CI's lint:baseline now fails on any NEW a11y warning (new code must associate labels / label controls / key-handle interactive elements), and the existing debt can only ratchet down as files are touched. Counts captured fresh on main, no eslintcache. This is the enforced answer to 'don't leave a11y warnings just because they're pre-existing' — new ones are blocked, old ones must shrink.

a11y-paydown-1.rationale: label-has-for 743 -> 474. Two parts: (1) corrected the rule to WCAG-standard `required: { some: [nesting, id] }` in eslint.config.js — the plugin default `every` over-flagged 260 already-accessible labels (associated by for/id OR nesting) as false positives; `some` flags only genuinely unassociated labels. (2) Real paydown: UploadDocumentModal associated all 9 label/control pairs via for/id (no DOM restructure), clearing 9 label-has-for + 9 form-control-has-label (593 -> 584). The remaining 474 + 584 stay gated and ratchet down in subsequent batches.

a11y-paydown-2.rationale: label-has-for 474 -> 27, form-control-has-label 584 -> 45 (986 real associations). Multi-agent swarm: one subagent per file across 137 files added matching for/id (or :aria-label when a control had no label element but a clear existing i18n key) — for/id/aria attributes only, no DOM/text/logic changes (verified by a diff scan + green build). The 72 remaining are genuine edge cases the agents correctly refused to force: labels for <button> toggle-groups / @click div-grids / custom components (not labelable via for), v-for fields needing a dynamic :id binding (a logic change), color-picker hex companions, and headless components (Checkbox/InputLabel) with no in-file control or i18n key. Those need targeted structural fixes (fieldset/legend, dynamic :id, component `for` prop) — a deliberate follow-up, not a blanket pass.

a11y-paydown-3.rationale: label-has-for 27 -> 1, form-control-has-label 45 -> 6 (65 sites). Triage swarm classified the 72 into 5 strategies; a fixer swarm (1 agent/file, 41 files) applied the routed fixes — existing-key sr-only <label>/aria-label (every key grep-confirmed in lang/en, the guard added after PR #121's hallucinated-key failure), dynamic :id for v-for fields, and group-aria (mis-used <label> -> <span id> + role=group aria-labelledby) for button/div-grid/custom-component groups. Verified: lang:coverage missing=0, green build, diff-scan clean. The final 7 (5 needing brand-new i18n keys, 2 shared headless components Checkbox/InputLabel needing a for/id/aria prop) are gated here and finished in batch 4 with the full test suite.

a11y-paydown-4.rationale: label-has-for 1 -> 0, form-control-has-label 6 -> 0 — DONE. The final 7 done by hand (precision over a swarm): 5 controls (Reports/Builder filter field/op/value, LegalHolds subject-type, InboxTab status) got :aria-label backed by 5 NEW i18n keys added to en+sw+ar at matching positions (parity held, lang:coverage missing=0); the 2 shared headless components got an additive optional prop — Checkbox an :aria-label (callers pass the name), InputLabel a `for` prop bound :for (member-accessed as props.for to dodge the JS reserved word). Build compiles every caller. Both rules now permanently locked at 0 — the ratchet blocks any new label/control-association warning across the app. Net over 4 batches: label-has-for 743 -> 0, form-control-has-label 593 -> 0.
